narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away feels like a door slamming shut just as you reach for the handle. Our model looked at your profile and predicted a 50% chance of not passing the bar exam, which is why we had to make this tough call. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 1, the lowest in your school, signaling to the model that your performance dipped significantly by the end, pulling hardest toward this risky outlook. Your LSAT score of 27 reinforced that concern, as it's on the lower side and the model saw it as a strong sign of potential struggles ahead. Layered on top was your Year 1 decile of 2, another early warning that grades haven't been your stronghold. Your Asian background, low family income, and undergraduate GPA of 2.6 each added to the picture of hurdles the model associated with bar success. Even being part-time and female factored in as subtle drags, based on patterns it's learned from thousands of similar paths. Together, these painted a story of someone facing an uphill battle, not impossible, but one where the odds tilt just enough against passing that we couldn't take the chance. It's not about your worth or potential—it's about patterns that help us protect spots for those the model sees as safer bets. If you boost that LSAT or strengthen your later grades, doors could open wider next time. You've got fire in you; channel it wisely. Hang in there.
